items.title: Promote notebook logic into a reusable module
items.scenario: A notebook cell holds a 60-line chunker; you want to use it from a pipeline and a CLI.
items.explanation: Move it to `ragapp/chunker.py`. Define `__all__ = ['chunk_text']`. Now the notebook does `from ragapp.chunker import chunk_text`; the pipeline imports the same; the CLI works via `python -m ragapp.chunker`.

items.title: Defer model loading until first call
items.scenario: `tiktoken.get_encoding(...)` at module top-level made `pytest` startup take 4 seconds.
items.explanation: Lazy-load inside the function (or via a module-level `functools.cache`d helper). Importing the module is now instant; tests start in milliseconds.

items.title: Inject the embedder instead of importing it
items.scenario: `chunker.py` directly imports `OpenAI()` from `openai`; tests can't run without API keys.
items.explanation: Take `embedder` as a parameter. Production passes `OpenAI()`; tests pass a fake. No more module-level singleton; module imports cheaply and tests don't need real API keys.

items.title: Sub-package for a swappable backend
items.scenario: You want to support both FAISS and BM25 retrieval; the rest of the code shouldn't care.
items.explanation: `ragapp/retrieval/` with `faiss_backend.py`, `bm25_backend.py`, and an `__init__.py` exporting a common `Retriever` interface. Callers do `from ragapp.retrieval import Retriever`; pick implementation via config.

items.title: Dual-use file with `if __name__ == '__main__'`
items.scenario: Same chunker module needs to be usable as a CLI for quick experiments.
items.explanation: Wrap the CLI in `_cli()` and call it under the `__main__` guard. `from ragapp.chunker import chunk_text` works as before; `python -m ragapp.chunker doc.txt` runs the CLI.
